serviceNotes.dust filter: Mercedes-Benz EQ maintenance booklet lists cabin (dust) filter replacement on a time and distance schedule similar to other current passenger cars (commonly every 24 months / 30,000 km in EU documentation).
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Brake fluid remains hygroscopic on EVs; Mercedes-Benz specifies two-year renewal on current EQ models per European operator and service literature pattern.
serviceNotes.check brake pad thickness: High regenerative braking reduces pad wear but periodic thickness checks are still required in the manufacturer inspection programme.
serviceNotes.high-voltage battery coolant: Traction battery thermal management coolant is specified in the EQ high-voltage maintenance supplement; renewal is typically required at long mileage (often around 120,000 km or time-based—confirm for your VIN).
serviceNotes.electric drivetrain coolant: E-motor and power electronics cooling circuits use dedicated coolant with long but finite service life; Mercedes EQ service data commonly aligns major coolant work near 120,000 km—verify workshop bulletin.
serviceNotes.reduction gear oil: Single-speed reduction gear oil is often labelled inspection-only officially; many specialists recommend fluid renewal around 120,000 km for long-term bearing health on EV drive units.
serviceNotes.coolant flush: If separate circuits are not renewed together, a full coolant exchange around 120,000 km restores additive packages for ancillary cooling loops.
